# hyperfluid_managed_postgresql_user (Resource)

A PostgreSQL role within a managed cluster. Import id is "<cluster_id>/<user_id>".

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hyperfluid_managed_postgresql_user" "editor" {
  managed_postgresql = hyperfluid_managed_postgresql.db.id
  username           = "app_editor"
  permission_level   = "editor"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `managed_postgresql` (String) Cluster id this user belongs to. Changing this forces a new user.
- `username` (String) Postgres role name. Changing this forces a new user.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `permission_level` (String) Permission level: editor or viewer.
- `tags` (List of String) User-defined tags.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `phase` (String) Current lifecycle phase.
- `slug` (String) Derived slug.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Managed PostgreSQL user import id is "<cluster_id>/<user_id>".
terraform import hyperfluid_managed_postgresql_user.editor 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111
```
